channel.title: 2026-05-21 AI × Science 文献日报 RSS
channel.description: 2026-05-21 当天筛选出的 AI × 物理 / 化学 / 材料交叉文献 RSS。

item.title: How accurate are foundational machine learning interatomic potentials for heterogeneous catalysis?
item.description:
📚 The Journal of Chemical Physics Current Issue
Foundational machine-learning interatomic potentials (MLIPs) are being developed at a rapid pace, promising closer and closer approximation to ab initio accuracy. This unlocks the possibility to simulate much larger length and time scales. However, benchmarks for these MLIPs are usually limited to ordered, crystalline, and bulk materials. Hence, reported performance does not necessarily reflect MLIP performance accurately in real applications such as heterogeneous catalysis. Here, we systematically analyze zero-shot performance of 80 different MLIPs, evaluating tasks typical for heterogeneous catalysis across a range of different datasets, including adsorption and reaction on surfaces of alloyed metals, oxides, and metal–oxide interfacial systems. We demonstrate that current-generation fo…

item.title: Representability-Aware Neural Networks for Reduced Density Matrices: Application to Fractional Chern Insulators
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Justin B. Hart, Awwab A. Azam, Thomas Li, Yunxuan Li, Ye Bi, Haining Pan, Jiabin Yu
arXiv:2605.20326v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We develop a representability-aware and interpolable neural network (NN) framework for predicting two-particle reduced density matrices (2-RDMs). The NN incorporates a subset of representability conditions through its architecture and loss function, and can operate on different momentum meshes, enabling evaluating the representability conditions across multiple meshes, which we call interpolated representability condition. The framework can be used either to predict 2-RDMs on large momentum meshes by interpolating exact results from small meshes, or as a variational 2-RDM ansatz optimized by energy minimization on arbitrary meshes. We apply this approach to the fractional Chern insulator in the one-band projected model of twisted bilayer MoT…

item.title: Connectivity determines the capability of sparse neural network quantum states
item.description:
📚 Machine Learning: Science and Technology
👤 Brandon Barton, Juan Carrasquilla, Christopher Roth and Agnes Valenti
The lottery ticket hypothesis (LTH) posits that within overparametrized neural networks, there exist sparse subnetworks that are capable of matching the performance of the original model when trained in isolation from the original initialization. We extend this hypothesis to the unsupervised task of approximating the ground state of quantum many-body Hamiltonians, a problem equivalent to finding a neural-network compression of the lowest-lying eigenvector of an exponentially large matrix. Focusing on two representative quantum Hamiltonians, the transverse field Ising model and the toric code, we demonstrate that sparse neural networks can reach accuracies comparable to their dense counterparts, even when pruned by more than an order of magnitude in parameter count. Crucially, and unlike t…

item.title: Ductile Mg-Te-Pb thermoelectric materials with ultralow lattice thermal conductivity predicted by a deep learning potential model
item.description:
📚 npj Computational Materials
👤 Xin-Xuan Wang, Zhen-Shuai Lei, Wen-Juan Li, Xiao-Bin Feng, Gang Chen, Peng-Cheng Zhai 等9位作者
npj Computational Materials, Published online: 21 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41524-026-02149-z Ductile Mg-Te-Pb thermoelectric materials with ultralow lattice thermal conductivity predicted by a deep learning potential model

item.title: The diffusion-driven orthorhombic to tetragonal transition in YBa 2 Cu 3 O 7 derived with a machine learning interatomic potential
item.description:
📚 npj Quantum Materials
👤 Davide Gambino, Niccolò Di Eugenio, Jesper Byggmästar, Johan Klarbring, Daniele Torsello, Flyura Djurabekova 等7位作者
npj Quantum Materials, Published online: 21 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41535-026-00891-7 The diffusion-driven orthorhombic to tetragonal transition in YBa 2 Cu 3 O 7 derived with a machine learning interatomic potential

item.title: Anisotropic Crystallization Kinetics and Interfacial Dynamics of Phase-Change Material Sb₂S₃ from Machine Learning Force Field Simulations
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Souvik Chakraborty, Wen-Qing Li, Yun Liu
arXiv:2605.20785v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The phase-change material antimony sulfide (Sb₂S₃₎ relies on rapid and reversible phase transitions between crystalline and amorphous states, which are critical for their performance in data storage and photonics applications. In this work, a machine learning force field is developed based on the moment tensor potential approach, allowing us to understand the atomistic origin of the structural evolution and crystallization kinetics in Sb₂S₃ for the first time, by enabling large-scale molecular dynamics simulations (up to 7680 atoms for 40 ns). Sb₂S₃ shows anisotropic growth rates with the [100] facet exhibiting the fastest growth due to the strong Sb-S covalent bonding along its quasi-1D ribbon-like structure of its crystalline phase. The ac…

item.title: Dataset-aware entropy-maximized active learning for machine-learned interatomic potentials
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Meiyan Wang, Rishi Rao, Li Zhu
arXiv:2605.20384v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present an active learning framework for efficiently generating training data for machine-learned interatomic potentials (MLIPs). The method combines local entropy-driven molecular dynamics with global dataset-aware filtering: a per-configuration entropy term biases MD trajectories toward structurally diverse snapshots, while a global entropy measure, the log-determinant of the fingerprint covariance matrix of the entire dataset, selects only those configurations that provide genuinely new information. We employ dual covariance modes (per-atom for disordered structures and per-config for ordered phases) to achieve broad coverage of configuration space. Combined with a pre-trained foundation model (Allegro-OAM-L) and analytical fingerprint…

item.title: Modeling phase separation in polymer-derived carbonitride ceramics through extended machine learning molecular dynamics
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Fabien Mortier, Sylvian Cadars, Olivier Masson, Mauro Boero, Guido Ori, Yun Wang, Samuel Bernard, Assil Bouzid
arXiv:2605.20358v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Polymer-derived ceramics combine the thermal stability of ceramics with the versatile properties of carbon domains, but modeling their atomic-scale evolution during processing remains elusive due to the limitations of traditional computational methods. To address this issue, here we develop and apply a machine learning interatomic potential for silicon carbonitride-based (Si-C-N-H) systems, trained on a diversified database of over 9000 configurations -including amorphous models, high-temperature states, surfaces, and crystal structure predictions - to capture the full complexity of these materials. This potential enables large-scale molecular dynamics simulations of 8000-atom systems revealing the atomic-scale evolution of the polymer-deriv…

item.title: Physics-informed convolutional neural networks for fluid flow through porous media
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Rafał Topolnicki, Paweł Dłotko, Maciej Matyka
arXiv:2605.20250v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Accurate simulation of fluid flow in porous media is challenging due to complex pore-space geometries and the computational cost of solving the Navier-Stokes equations. This difficulty is particularly important when repeated simulations are required, as standard numerical solvers may converge slowly in intricate porous domains. We present a neural-network-based framework for predicting pore-scale velocity fields directly from sample geometry. The method uses a convolutional encoder-decoder architecture with skip connections to preserve spatial detail while extracting multi-scale features. Physical consistency is encouraged through a custom loss function combining velocity reconstruction with incompressibility, no-flow conditions inside sol…

item.title: Transferable 3D Convolutional Neural Networks for Elastic Constants Prediction in Nanoporous Metals
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Sergei Zorkaltsev, Rafał Topolnicki, Tal-El Carmon, Santhosh Mathesan, Paweł Dłotko, Dan Mordehai, Maciej Harańczyk
arXiv:2605.20890v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The topology of nanoporous metals is crucial for determining their mechanical response. In this work, we generated 6,000 gold and 422 silver nanoporous structures and calculated three components of elastic modulus with Molecular Dynamics simulations, resulting in 19,263 data points. This study compared two distinct approaches of predicting elastic modulus: a Fully-Connected neural network trained on precomputed topological descriptors, and several 3D Convolutional neural network architectures adapted from computer vision. The 3D CNNs outperformed the descriptor-based baseline model (R² = 0.704), with to-performing DenseNet-201 architecture achieving R² = 0.955. Additionally, the effects of training grid resolution, dataset size, and descript…

item.title: Label-free SERS Discrimination of Native Proline Hydroxylation at Single-molecule peptide by Deep Learning-assisted plasmonic nanopore
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yingqi Zhao, Kuo Zhan, Pei-Lin Xin, Yuge Liang, Enock Adjei Agyekum, Matti Putkonen, Shuai Li, Francesco De Angelis, Jianan Huang
arXiv:2605.21084v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Post-translational modifications (PTMs) play essential roles in regulating protein structure, function, and cellular signalling. However, peptide level discrimination of hydroxylation at the single-molecule level remains difficult. Here, we report a particle-in-pore single-molecule surface-enhanced Raman spectroscopy (SERS) platform combined with peak occurrence frequency (POF) analysis and a one-dimensional convolutional neural network (1D-CNN) for discriminating hydroxylated and non-hydroxylated HIF peptide fragments. Three peptide pairs containing the Pro-564 hydroxylation site, with lengths of 7, 9, and 15 amino acids (AAs), were investigated. POF analysis revealed reproducible hydroxylation-dependent spectral changes in the 7AA and 9AA…

item.title: Light‐Modulated Xylan‐Reinforced Nanofluidic Memristor for Ionic Neural Network‐Based Robot Movement Modulation
item.description:
📚 Wiley: Advanced Materials: Table of Contents
👤 Guanghui Song, Hao Zhou, Zehui Li, Xiuru Pan, Ziwen Lv, Jun Rao, Xiang Hao, Baozhong Lü, Gegu Chen, Yupeng Chen, Feng Peng
Advanced Materials, EarlyView.

item.title: A Simple but Efficient Transformer-Based Physics-Informed Neural Network for Incompressible Navier--Stokes Equations
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Biswanath Barman, Debdeep Chatterjee, Rajendra K. Ray
arXiv:2601.03613v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Traditional computational fluid dynamics and physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) often suffer from high computational cost, mesh sensitivity, and reduced accuracy for strongly nonlinear and time-dependent flows. To address these limitations, we propose PhysicsFormer, a simple and efficient Transformer-based physics-informed neural network framework for complex fluid flow simulations. The proposed architecture employs encoder--decoder multi-head attention to capture long-range temporal dependencies and enhance spatio-temporal information propagation. Unlike conventional multilayer perceptron-based PINNs, PhysicsFormer utilizes pseudo-sequential spatio-temporal representations together with a dynamics-weighted loss formulation to impr…

item.title: BALLAST: Bayesian Active Learning with Look-ahead Amendment for Sea-drifter Trajectories under Spatio-Temporal Vector Fields
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Rui-Yang Zhang, Lachlan Astfalck, Edward Cripps, David S. Leslie, Henry B. Moss
arXiv:2509.26005v4 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We introduce a formal active learning methodology for guiding the placement of Lagrangian observers to infer time-dependent vector fields -- a key task in oceanography, marine science, and ocean engineering -- using a physics-informed spatio-temporal Gaussian process surrogate model. The majority of existing placement campaigns either follow standard `space-filling' designs or relatively ad-hoc expert opinions. A key challenge to applying principled active learning in this setting is that Lagrangian observers are continuously advected through the vector field, so they make measurements at different locations and times. It is, therefore, important to consider the likely future trajectories of placed observers to account for the utility of…

item.title: Data-Efficient Neural Operator Training via Physics-Based Active Learning
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Alicja Polanska, Lorenzo Zanisi, Vignesh Gopakumar, Stanislas Pamela
arXiv:2605.21348v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Solving partial differential equations with neural operators significantly reduces computational costs but remains bottlenecked by high training data requirements. Active learning offers a natural framework to mitigate this by selectively acquiring the most informative samples in an iterative manner. We introduce physics-based acquisition - a novel physics-informed active learning algorithm that leverages the partial differential equation residual to guide data selection. We validate the method by presenting numerical experiments for the 1D Burgers equation and the 2D compressible Navier-Stokes equations. We show that, in our experiments, physics-based acquisition consistently outperforms random acquisition and matches the state of the art…

item.title: 基于PUBagging、堆叠集成与物理知情机器学习的新疆滑坡敏感性不确定性感知制图
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Yu Yang, Lei Yang, Fuyou Liu, Zhanwen Cao, Feng Ji, Marjan Maroufi 等7位作者
该研究提出融合混合背景采样、正-未标记（PU）Bagging降噪及分层建模策略的滑坡敏感性制图流程，涵盖基线机器学习模型、堆叠集成与物理知情学习三个层级。以地形、湿度、植被、曲率和土壤属性等12个因子为条件变量，比较逻辑回归、随机森林、XGBoost和神经网络等基线模型，并通过软投票与堆叠泛化优化。结果表明树型集成基线性能最强，堆叠集成整体判别能力最佳，物理知情XGBoost通过引入无限边坡稳定性代理变量实现物理一致性约束。

item.title: Huge ultrafast spin Seebeck effect mediated by laser-excited superdiffusive magnon currents
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Luca Mikadze, Peter M. Oppeneer, Markus Weißenhofer
arXiv:2605.21389v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Subpicosecond laser excitation of ferromagnetic metals induces strongly nonequilibrium dynamics involving scattering and transport of electrons, phonons, and magnons. Widely used theoretical approaches, such as the three-temperature model and diffusion equations, are ill-suited to capture these processes on ultrafast timescales. Here, we present an ab initio-parameterized microscopic framework that incorporates nonthermal magnon scattering and transport via the quantum Boltzmann equation. We apply this approach to simulate ultrafast laser-induced demagnetization in bcc Fe films. The model predicts an ultrafast spin Seebeck effect, characterized by a strong burst of fast-moving magnonic spin current reaching technologically relevant amplitude…

item.title: Hybrid Improper Ferroelectricity and Moiré Superlattices-induced Exciton Quantization in Layered 2D Halide Perovskite
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Sanika S. Padelkar, Sharidya Rahman, Mattia Belotti, Naufan Nurrosyid, Craig Forsyth, Alasdair Mckay, Tam Nguyen, Thi Vu Mung, Lan Nguyen, Naeimeh Mozaffari, Alexandr N. Simonov, Aftab Alam, Jacek J. Jasieniak
arXiv:2605.21449v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: 2D Ruddlesden-Popper perovskites are compelling platforms for quantum-confined optoelectronics. However, polar order in iodide composition remains rare under ambient conditions, and the mechanistic origin of anomalous photoluminescence in this class of perovskite is still speculative. Here, we demonstrate that solution-grown (PA)₂FAPb₂I₇ single crystals develop an inadvertent moiré superlattice through pseudo-merohedral twinning, driven by hybrid improper ferroelectricity in which trilinear mode coupling between two primary zone-boundary modes (X₂⁺ and X₃⁻⁾ and a secondary Γ₄⁻ polar displacement simultaneously breaks inversion symmetry and imposes a ca. 5.17deg rotational misalignment between adjacent layers. This symmetry breaking activates…

item.title: Comment on "Instability of the ferromagnetic quantum critical point and symmetry of the ferromagnetic ground state in two-dimensional and three-dimensional electron gases with arbitrary spin-orbit splitting"
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 D. Belitz, T. R. Kirkpatrick
arXiv:2601.19959v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Metallic quantum ferromagnets in the absence of quenched disorder are known to generically undergo a first-order quantum phase transition, avoiding the quantum critical point that had originally been expected. This is due to soft modes in the underlying Fermi liquid that lead to long-ranged correlations. These correlations in turn yield a nonanalytic dependence of the free energy on the magnetization even at a mean-field level that results in a fluctuation-induced first-order transition. Kirkpatrick and Belitz [Phys. Rev. Lett. bf 124, 147201 (2020)] have pointed out that one notable exception are non-centrosymmetric metals with a strong spin-orbit interaction. In such materials the spin-orbit interaction gives the relevant soft modes a…

item.title: 磁性拓扑绝缘体多层中的设计量子态
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Deyi Zhuo, Han Tay, Cui-Zu Chang
该综述总结过去十年通过分子束外延设计磁性拓扑绝缘体多层的实验进展。将磁性掺杂与非掺杂TI层视为拓扑乐高积木，讨论如何利用层厚、磁性掺杂、异质结构架构和堆垛序列以原子层精度控制磁交换能隙、层间耦合和陈数C。涵盖C=1量子反常霍尔效应的实现、磁性调制掺杂三层中的高陈数量子反常霍尔态、工程化平台相变、介观器件及电学开关等进展。

item.title: 面向高效自旋-轨道力矩开关的磁性拓扑绝缘体三层设计
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Ling-Jie Zhou, Deyi Zhuo, Han Tay, Zi-Jie Yan, Pu Xiao, Xiaoda Liu, Bomin Zhang, Cui-Zu Chang
该研究利用分子束外延在热处理SrTiO₃(111)衬底上合成系列层厚可控的磁性拓扑绝缘体三层结构。电输运测量揭示，自旋-轨道力矩驱动磁化反转及边缘电流手性转换受SrTiO₃(111)衬底诱导的充电效应调控，该效应在上下磁性TI层间产生非对称化学势排列。通过异质结构设计、栅压和面内磁场可调控开关极性与效率，确认化学势非对称性是高开关比的起源。

item.title: 铁电KNbO₃纳米片的热驱动热催化析氢与染料降解
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Salma Touili, Bouchra Asbani, Youness Hadouch, Mbarek Amjoud, Daoud Mezzane, Nejc Suban, Hana Ursic, Nitul S. Rajput, Zdravko Kutnjak, Brigita Rozic, Mustapha Jouiad, Mimoun El Marssi
利用正交铁电铌酸钾（KNbO₃）纳米片实现高效热催化析氢和罗丹明B降解。在20–50°C热循环下，30次循环后产氢量达680 μmol g⁻¹；16次循环后RhB去除率达84%。优异性能归因于KNbO₃纳米片强自发极化和优异热释电性质促进的电荷产生与界面反应。

item.title: 平面腔中双层磁性薄膜的几何可控磁子-极化激元
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 S. Solihin, Ahmad R. T. Nugraha, Muhammad Aziz Majidi
该工作构建双层平面微波腔模型，推导双磁性薄膜的完整散射理论。研究表明磁子-光子耦合依赖于空间构型而非仅总磁体积：波腹兼容位置增强耦合，波节兼容位置抑制耦合；弱对称破缺可将暗模激活为额外分支。对于非零交换作用，简化多模理论预言奇数驻自旋波模式存在家族分辨的亮通道与暗通道。

item.title: 缺碱K₁₋ₓCrTe₂（x≈0.3）单晶的面内铁磁性与临界动力学
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Catherine Witteveen, Felix Eder, Sara A. López-Paz, Vladimir Pomjakushin, Jonas A. Krieger, Zurab Guguchia, Harald O. Jeschke, Martin Månsson, Fabian O. von Rohr
该工作报道缺碱K₁₋ₓCrTe₂（x≈0.3）单晶的合成与表征，其结晶于Cm空间群，具有三角棱柱配位的K⁺，区别于更化学计量比ACrX₂化合物的八面体环境。磁化测量显示在T_C=117 K处发生尖锐铁磁转变，并具有面内磁各向异性，得到密度泛函理论支持。中子衍射揭示层内和层间Cr自旋的铁磁排列，这与LiCrTe₂和NaCrTe₂的面外A型反铁磁性形成对比，但与CrTe₂相似。交流磁化率和μSR表明T_C以上存在短程序、以下存在动力学行为，为研究二维磁体中自旋取向和维度性提供了可调平台。

item.title: 交替磁性自旋劈裂的配体介导起源
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Luigi Camerano, Federico Bisti, Gianni Profeta
该研究以g波交替磁体Co₁/₄NbSe₂为对象，结合第一性原理计算与Wannier哈密顿量工程，揭示了交替磁性自旋劈裂的微观成键机制。研究表明该劈裂源于配体介导的杂化而非直接磁离子跃迁，建立了最小模型与对称性指导的材料搜索之间的微观桥梁。

item.title: 呼吸烧绿石尖晶石AgInCr₄S₈单晶的磁性
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Andrew F. May, Christopher M. Pasco, V. O. Garlea, Karolina Gornicka, Matthias D. Frontzek, Xiaoping Wang, Pyeongjae Park, Andrew D. Christianson
该工作通过化学气相输运生长AgInCr₄S₈单晶，X射线和中子衍射证实Ag/In的结晶有序化导致Cr³⁺形成呼吸烧绿石构型。在T_N≈9.6 K以下观察到长程反铁磁有序。比热数据显示9.5 K附近小的λ异常，估计的磁熵在3T_N时达到预期值的约1/3，表明顺磁相中存在显著短程序。单晶中子衍射证实传播矢量k=(0,0,δ)、δ=0.343（5 K）的非公度自旋结构；最小模型由Cr原子的铁磁层组成，磁矩位于层面内并在垂直方向调制形成沿k传播的螺旋结构。

item.title: 电纺CFO-BCTSn纳米复合材料的多铁性及其磁电与磁场传感应用
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Youness Hadouch, Nayad Abdallah, Daoud Mezzane, Mbarek Amjoud, Voicu Dolocan, Khalid Hoummada, Nikola Novak, Anna Razumnaya, Brigita Rozic, Val Fisinger, Hana Ursic, Valentin Laguta, Zdravko Kutnjak, Mimoun El Marssi
该研究采用溶胶-凝胶电纺法制备多铁CFO-BCTSn复合纳米纤维。电子显微镜显示纤维直径为120-150 nm；X射线衍射、拉曼光谱和高分辨透射电镜证实尖晶石CFO相和钙钛矿BCTSn相共存且无可见次生相。磁滞回线测量展示纳米纤维的磁性行为，压电力显微镜确认其压电性；电学极化与未极化样品的磁滞回线差异证明磁电耦合。这些无铅复合纳米纤维在纳米尺度磁电器件和磁场传感应用中展现出潜力。

item.title: 多层3R-MoS₂铁电翻转的剪切模拉曼成像
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yulu Liu, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Xiaoxiang Xi
该工作利用剪切模拉曼成像追踪多层3R-MoS₂中的铁电翻转。单个薄片内机械分割区域独立响应并遵循不同路径；部分极化的终态表明畴壁可驻留在选定层对之间，产生部分堆垛转变。中间态的停留时间变化广泛，表明钉扎位点强烈影响动力学。二次谐波产生测量进一步揭示三种特征样品边界和畴壁取向，包括zigzag-armchair角平分线附近普遍的手性方向。这些结果为原型滑移铁电中畴壁介导翻转提供了直接、非侵入的视角，并确定钉扎和剥离产生的边界是调控其动力学的关键因素。

item.title: 原子级薄铁磁体中的磁子流体动力学
item.description:
📚 AAAS: Science: Table of Contents
👤 Ruolan Xue, Nikola Maksimovic, Pavel E. Dolgirev, Li-Qiao Xia, Aaron Müller, Ryota Kitagawa, Francisco Machado, Dahlia R. Klein, David MacNeill, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Pablo Jarillo-Herrero, Mikhail D. Lukin, Eugene Demler, Amir Yacoby
该工作在原子级薄铁磁体中实验研究了磁子流体动力学行为，揭示了二维极限下磁子输运的集体激发特征。该研究为理解低维磁性体系中的非平衡磁子动力学提供了重要实验依据。

item.title: 交错磁性的对称性分类与二维IV型磁性的涌现
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Lett.
👤 Mu Tian, Chaoxi Cui, Zeying Zhang, Jingyi Duan, Wanxiang Feng, and Run-Wu Zhang
二维交错磁性（AM）因其优异物理性质和广泛应用前景备受关注，但其分类在从三维降至二维时发生根本性范式转变。该工作系统研究了二维交错磁性的对称性分类框架，揭示了传统三维分类方案在低维极限下的失效机制，并预言了新型IV型磁性的存在。该研究为二维量子磁性材料的设计与物性调控提供了理论基础。

item.title: Enhanced Reinforcement Learning-based Process Synthesis via Quantum Computing
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Austin Braniff (Department of Chemical and Biomedical Engineering, West Virginia University), Fengqi You (R.F. Smith School of Chemical and Biomolecular Engineering, Cornell University), Yuhe Tian (Department of Chemical and Biomedical Engineering, West Virginia University)
arXiv:2605.21213v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: In this work, we present quantum reinforcement learning (RL) as a solution strategy for process synthesis problems. Building on our prior work, we develop a generalized framework that formally poses process synthesis as a Markov decision process and introduces quantum-enhanced RL algorithms to solve it with improved scalability. Earlier implementations of quantum-based RL for process synthesis were limited by qubit requirements, which scaled poorly with problem complexity. This work overcomes this challenge by introducing state encoding algorithms to decouple qubit requirements from problem size. A classical RL-based solution strategy is used as a baseline to benchmark the quantum algorithms under identical training conditions. All algorit…

item.title: High Performance TiO2 Ferroelectric Field Effect Transistors with HfZrO2 for Neuromorphic Computing
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Chandan Samanta, Elia Palmese, Ziyu Ouyang, Tuofu Zhama, Robinson Pino, Yuping Zeng
arXiv:2605.20406v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: TiO2 ferroelectric field effect transistors (FeFETs) with HfZrO2 (HZO) ferroelectric dielectric layers and bottom gate topology are fabricated for applications in neuromorphic systems. Two sets of devices are fabricated with different gate topologies by varying the thickness of the ferroelectric gate stack. Different device architectures are studied by varying the source drain length (LSD) and gate length (LG). The devices have high on/off ratios up to 10⁷ with low leakage off currents <10⁻¹² A. Repeated cycle testing shows high reliability and a stable memory window. The devices have large memory windows ranging from 3 to 8 V.

item.title: Room‐Temperature Skyrmionic Synapse in 2D Ferromagnet Fe3GaTe2 Operating via Collective Spin Texture Transformation
item.description:
📚 Wiley: Advanced Materials: Table of Contents
👤 Jixiang Huang, Tongji Zhu, PeiYu Cai, Xiaoming Ma, Zhen Wang, Ruifu Zhang, Yi Hao, Jingdi Lu, Liubing He, Jun Xu, Yuejie Zhang, Wanjun Jiang, Jing Tao, Shiming Lei, Pavel Parchinskiy, Jing Teng, Lingfei Wang, Wei Niu, Elton J.G. Santos, Xiaoqian Zhang, Peng Li
Advanced Materials, EarlyView.

item.title: Stable Analog Weight Programming in Single‐Crystalline van der Waals Ferroelectric Transistors for Reliable Computing‐in‐Memory
item.description:
📚 Wiley: Advanced Materials: Table of Contents
👤 Mengjiao Li, Feng‐Shou Yang, Yanan Liu, Chi Zhang, Enlong Li, Jingbo Yang, Che‐Yi Lin, Hefei Liu, Chen Luo, Hsing‐Chicn Chien, Yuan‐Ming Chang, Jan‐Chi Yang, Chang‐Hong Shen, Yu‐Lun Chueh, Jun Li, Jianhua Zhang, Jiunn‐Lin Wu, Po‐Wen Chiu, Yen‐Fu Lin
Advanced Materials, EarlyView.

item.title: 作为磁振子透镜的反铁磁斯格明子
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Hongbin Wu (武宏斌), Zi-Wu Wang (王子武), and Jin Lan (兰金)
该研究将磁织构中的自旋波散射描述为赝磁场中的磁振子偏转，并分离磁拓扑与Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya（DM）相互作用对该场的各自贡献。分析表明当DM相互作用占主导时，赝磁场将磁振子偏转分裂为双光束，使反铁磁斯格明子成为内置磁振子透镜，实现自旋波的聚焦与准直。

item.title: Ba₄SbRu₃O₁₂：几何阻挫网络上孤立磁性团簇的六方钙钛矿
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Materials
👤 Emma A. Pollock, Rabindranath Bag, Lalit Yadav, Sara Haravifard, and Patrick M. Woodward
Ba₄SbRu₃O₁₂结晶结构由三个共面RuO₆八面体组成的团簇（Ru₃O₁₂）通过SbO₆八面体连接构成。基于X射线和中子粉末衍射数据的Rietveld精修确定了其晶体结构，揭示了该材料中磁性团簇在几何阻挫晶格上的孤立分布特征。该化合物为研究阻挫量子磁性和低维磁激发行为提供了新的实验平台。

item.title: 对称性破缺对CrSb交错磁性的影响及碎片化节点曲线的形成
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Arindom Das, Arijit Mandal, Nayana Devaraj, and B. R. K. Nanda
该工作以典型交错磁体CrSb为例，探索了动量空间自旋极化形成的微观机制，重点研究了对称性破缺如何导致交错磁性特征的改变以及碎片化节点曲线的出现。该研究为理解交错磁体的非常规动量空间特性提供了新视角。

item.title: 亚铁磁绝缘体铽铁石榴石非共线相中自旋霍尔拓扑霍尔效应的出现
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Materials
👤 Mehak Loyal, Akashdeep Akashdeep, Edoardo Mangini, Edgar Galíndez-Ruales, Maja Eich, Nan Wang, Qianqian Lan, Lei Jin, Rafal Dunin-Borkowski, Timo Kuschel, Mathias Kläui, and Gerhard Jakob
稀土铁石榴石中的磁补偿为竞争子晶格磁矩产生非共线（倾斜）磁构型提供了独特环境。该工作研究了铽铁石榴石非共线相中自旋霍尔拓扑霍尔效应的出现，揭示了子晶格磁化强度彼此及与外场均不对齐时的输运特性。

item.title: 利用等离激元加热实现反铁磁体中的磁开关
item.description:
📚 Editors' Suggestions
👤 H. Y. Yuan, Yizheng Wu, and Olena Gomonay
该工作提出通过等离激元激发实现纳米尺度可控加热，以驱动反铁磁体中的磁信息处理。这一方案有望解决绿色信息技术中热耗散这一重大障碍，为低能耗磁存储与计算提供新思路。

item.title: (Hf,Mo)Fe₂系巡游电子铁磁性：自旋涨落理论分析
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 I. Sarr, S. El Bidaoui, O. Isnard, A. Verniere, and L. V. B. Diop
该研究系统研究伪二元Laves相材料(Hf,Mo)Fe₂中Fe亚晶格在顺磁态及磁有序态的磁性行为，采用自旋涨落理论进行分析。顺磁相分析揭示了该体系巡游电子铁磁性的特征，具体物理参数与相变行为需查阅原文。

item.title: 具有全进/全出磁序的5d过渡金属氧化物Y₂Ir₂O₇和Cd₂Os₂O₇中的压磁效应
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Hiroki Nanjo, Yoshinori Imai, Takuya Aoyama, Jun-ichi Yamaura, and Kenya Ohgushi
该工作研究了烧绿石型氧化物Y₂Ir₂O₇和Cd₂Os₂O₇中的压磁效应，这两种材料在低温下呈现非共面的全进/全出反铁磁序。研究揭示了非共面磁结构对压磁响应的影响机制。

item.title: 手性磁性薄膜中受限霍普夫子的前驱行为、袋形成与晶格不稳定性
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Research
👤 Andrey O. Leonov and Takayuki Shigenaga
霍普夫子作为三维拓扑粒子激发，其稳定机制与相行为远未得到充分探索。该工作通过研究手性磁性薄膜中受限霍普夫子的前驱行为、袋形成过程及晶格不稳定性，揭示了三维拓扑激发的形成动力学与相变特征。

item.title: 拓扑绝缘体异质结Cr₂Ge₂Te₆/(Bi,Sb)₂Te₃/Eu₃Fe₅O₁₂中的近邻诱导类亚铁磁界面
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Ko-Hsuan Mandy Chen, Hsuan-Ning Chen, Pei-Tze Chen, Tay-Rong Chang, Shang-Fan Lee, Minghwei Hong, and Jueinai Kwo
该工作报道了磁性拓扑绝缘体异质结构Cr₂Ge₂Te₆/(Bi,Sb)₂Te₃/Eu₃Fe₅O₁₂的磁输运性质，发现近邻效应可诱导出类亚铁磁特性的界面磁序。该研究为调控磁性拓扑绝缘体的拓扑态与输运性质提供了新途径。

item.title: 将自旋扭转为自旋波透镜
item.description:
📚 Physics
👤 Marric Stephens
该Physics新闻文章报道，材料中可控的磁织构可用于聚焦或准直自旋波，为未来自旋电子器件提供新途径。该工作围绕磁纹理对自旋波的调控机制展开，具体实验细节与性能参数需查阅原文。

item.title: Group-Algebraic Tensors: Provably-optimal Equivariant Learning and Physical Symmetry Discovery
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Paulina Hoyos, Shashanka Ubaru, Dongsung Huh, Vasileios Kalantzis, Kenneth L. Clarkson, Misha Kilmer, Haim Avron, Lior Horesh
arXiv:2605.20440v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce the star_G tensor algebra, in which any finite group G defines the multiplication rule, making equivariance an intrinsic algebraic property rather than an architectural constraint. The framework rests on three machine-verified theoretical pillars: (i)~an Eckart-Young optimality guarantee for the star_G-SVD: the first such result for symmetry-preserving tensor approximation, exact and polynomial-time; (ii)~a Kronecker factorization that composes multiple symmetries by replacing F_G with FG_₁ øtimes FG_₂ with no architectural redesign; and (iii)~a 600-line Lean~4 formalization of the star_G algebra. The framework provides capabilities that equivariant neural networks (ENNs) structurally cannot: a closed-form per-irreducible-repres…

item.title: Q-SYNTH：面向不平衡欺诈检测的混合量子-经典对抗增强
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Adam Innan, Mansour El Alami, Nouhaila Innan, Muhammad Shafique, Mohamed Bennai
该工作提出Q-SYNTH，一种混合经典-量子生成对抗框架，以参数化量子电路为生成器、经典神经网络为判别器，专门用于表格数据中的少数类欺诈样本合成。评估涵盖两个维度：对真实欺诈样本的统计保真度（基于Kolmogorov-Smirnov统计与Wasserstein距离的分布相似性、AUC-ROC衡量的真伪可检测性）及下游欺诈检测性能（量子与经典分类器）。实验表明，Q-SYNTH较经典GAN基线降低边缘分布失配，同时保持竞争力，为极端类别不平衡下的欺诈检测提供了量子增强的数据增强方案。

item.title: Orbital-Angular-Momentum Entangled Photon Emission from Circular Currents in Semiconductor-Superconductor Structures
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Avi Koriat, Ankit Kumar, Alex Hayat
arXiv:2605.20329v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: We theoretically demonstrate that a superconducting circular current induced in a semiconductor results in emission of orbital-angular-momentum (OAM) entangled photon pairs upon carrier recombination. Combining the macroscopic Ginzburg-Landau theory and the microscopic Bardeen-Cooper-Schrieffer (BCS) theory, we investigate the emission of a superconducting light-emitting diode (SLED) with a spatially varying phase profile in the superconducting order parameter. We show that in the active region of the SLED with a circular supercurrent, radiative recombination processes inherit the order parameter phase and result in photon pairs emitted into modes of different OAM quantum numbers. We demonstrate that coherent superposition of superconducti…

item.title: Ultra-Confinement of Polaritons in Single Atomic Layer Ag Photonic Quantum Dots
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Xinyi Li, Tetyana Ignatova, Chengye Dong, Krishnan Mekkanamkulam Ananthanarayanan, Rinu Abraham Maniyara, Arpit Jain, Furkan Turker, Vinay Kammarchedu, Aida Ebrahimi, Joshua A. Robinson, Slava V. Rotkin
arXiv:2605.21345v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Light scattering by two-dimensional (2D) van der Waals heterostructures (vdWHs) is immense, especially given their infinitesimal volume, thus enabling strong light-matter interactions. Surface 2D polariton waves manifest through large concentration of electromagnetic field in vertical direction, normal to their propagation. By confining vdWH materials into 2D photonic shapes, one can manipulate and compress light in lateral directions. Scattering-type scanning near-field optical microscopy is a perfect tool for direct imaging of the propagating polaritons and studying the properties of confined polaritons in nanostructures. Though, thus far the quantitative analysis, such the wavelength extraction, has been challenged for confined polaritons…

item.title: Neural operators for free-boundary problems
item.description:
📚 Nature Machine Intelligence
👤 Constantinos Siettos
Nature Machine Intelligence, Published online: 21 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s42256-026-01238-4 Free boundary problems, such as modelling glacier melt, are difficult to capture with neural operators. A new framework addresses this challenge by leveraging the mathematical principle of topological conjugacy.

item.title: 基于最优传输的跨个体几何fMRI激活字典学习
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Sonia Mazelet, Rémi Flamary, Bertrand Thirion
该工作提出一种新颖的fMRI字典学习方法，显式处理个体间脑几何变异性，避免传统模板投影导致的主体特异性信息丢失。方法采用基于最优传输的Fused Gromov-Wasserstein（FGW）距离比较不同几何与特征的图结构；针对fMRI大图的多重FGW距离计算瓶颈，利用摊销优化学习神经网络预测最优传输计划的近似，大幅降低计算成本。此外，学习依赖于FGW权衡参数的字典原子，以创建可解释且跨个体可比较的脑活动表征，支持脑状态分类与群体水平分析等下游任务。

item.title: Quantum End-to-End Learning for Contextual Combinatorial Optimization
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Jaehwan Lee, Changhyun Kwon
arXiv:2605.20222v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Contextual combinatorial optimization (CCO) plays a critical role in decision-making under uncertainty, yet remains a significant challenge. We present Quantum End-to-End Learning (QEL), the first quantum computing-based end-to-end learning framework for CCO that leverages Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithms. Inspired by the integration of state preparation and evolution in data re-uploading, we propose a context re-uploading phase-separator that jointly captures the complex relations among contexts, uncertain coefficients, and optimal solutions. This allows a contextual encoder to be seamlessly integrated within a quantum surrogate policy, enabling joint end-to-end training with a stationarity guarantee. Exploiting an optimization…

item.title: Surrogate models study for laser-plasma accelerator electron source design through numerical optimisation
item.description:
📚 Machine Learning: Science and Technology
👤 G Kane, P Drobniak, A Beck, S Kazamias, V Kubytskyi, M Lenivenko, B Lucas, F Massimo, J Serhal, A Specka and K Cassou
Designing a high-quality plasma injector electron source driven by a laser beam relies on numerical parametric studies using particle-in-cell (PIC) codes. The common input parameters to explore are laser characteristics, plasma species and density profiles produced by computational fluid dynamics studies. We demonstrate the construction of surrogate models (SMs) using machine learning techniques for a laser-plasma injector (LPI) based on more than 3000 PIC simulations of laser Wakefield acceleration performed for sparsely spaced input parameters published by Drobniak et al (2023Phys. Rev. Accel. Beams, 26 091302) . The SM developed in this article approximates a nonlinear mapping . SMs are highly relevant to the design and optimisation of LPI systems, as they enable the rapid mapping of a…

item.title: 混合经验-机器学习框架在海得拉巴拉吉夫·甘地国际机场低能见度概率预报中的应用
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 SRAVANI A, Prayek S, Lakshman Rao V, Siddartha v, Mallikarujun Reddy P, Anil N
该研究提出基于能见度潜力指数（VPI）与机器学习模型（逻辑回归、决策树、XGBoost）的混合预报框架，利用2017–2024年小时观测数据对拉吉夫·甘地国际机场低能见度（≤1000 m）及极低能见度（≤500 m）事件进行概率预测。VPI≥3与极低能见度事件概率显著相关，独立验证表明该框架可有效提升机场运行决策能力。

item.title: Benchmarking a machine-learning differential equations solver on a neutral-atom logical processor
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Pauline Mathiot, Elio Garnaoui, Axel-Ugo Leriche, Evan Philip, Boris Albrecht, Clémence Briosne-Fréjaville, Lorenzo Cardarelli, Antoine Cornillot, Gwennolé Cournez, Luc Couturier, Julius De Hond, Rebecca El Koussaifi, Thomas Eritzpokoff, Florian Fasola, Antonio Andrea Gentile, Casper Gyurik, Clotilde Hamot, Loïc Henriet, Gaétan Hercé, Michael Kaicher, Lucas Lassablière, François-Marie Le Régent, Edgar Leroux, Yohann Machu, Hadriel Mamann, Luis Ortiz, Annie Paine, Thomas Pansiot, Arnaud Peloquin, Francisco Ponciano, Julien Ripoll, Raja Selvarajan, Adrien Signoles, Henrique Silvério, Siddhy Tan, Marie Taouzinet, Selim Touati, Louis Vignoli, Antoine Browaeys, Pascal Scholl
arXiv:2605.21276v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: We report on a performance comparison between physical and logical computations on a prototypical machine-learning application: solving differential equations using quantum kernel methods. The algorithm is implemented on an atom-based logical quantum processor, both at the physical and logical levels. We show that the kernel estimated from the logical implementation performs better than its physical counterpart on relevant metrics. We observe how such performance improvement can be traced back to specific noise-induced errors detected by the chosen encoding. We apply the computed quantum kernel to the task of solving differential equations, confirming how the superior performance of a logical quantum kernel is retained also at an end-to-en…

item.title: 网络之网络上三态观点模型的平衡与动力学
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Irene Ferri, Albert Díaz-Guilera, Hiroki Sayama
作者研究了一种网络之网络上的三态观点模型，其中每个智能体具有内部信念图并通过外部社会图与其他智能体交互。每个信念可取两种对立极化态或中性态，中性参数调节中立立场的相对确信度；温度参数纳入外部社会激化和内部认知失调容忍度。通过解析方法和蒙特卡洛模拟，发现极化共识失稳的临界温度随附加结构参数变化，内部信念拓扑(一维链、团和星型结构)影响集体结果。

item.title: Supervised Latent Restructuring for Small-Data Quantum Learning in Plant Phenomics
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Alakananda Mitra, David H. Fleisher, Vangimalla Reddy, Chittaranjan Ray
arXiv:2605.20413v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: High-dimensional biological data often exhibit a severe mismatch between feature dimensionality and sample size, making reliable classification difficult in extremely small-data regimes. In these settings, kernel methods can lose discriminative power when latent compression fails to preserve class-separating structure. We study this problem in fine-grained plant phenomics and propose a hybrid workflow that compresses 1280-dimensional deep image embeddings into a 64-dimensional PCA space and then restructures them into an 11-dimensional supervised latent space using Linear Discriminant Analysis (LDA), followed by GPU-accelerated Quantum Kernel Alignment (QKA) on NVIDIA L40S hardware. Empirically, supervised latent restructuring substantially…

item.title: Dynamics of disordered quantum systems with two- and three-dimensional tensor networks
item.description:
📚 AAAS: Science: Table of Contents
👤 Joseph Tindall, Antonio Francesco Mello, Matthew Fishman, E. Miles Stoudenmire, Dries Sels
Science, Volume 392, Issue 6800, Page 868-872, May 2026.

item.title: Tree tensor network impurity solver based on Cayley-tree mapping
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Bo Zhan, Jia-Lin Chen, Zhen Fan, and Tao Xiang
Author(s): Bo Zhan, Jia-Lin Chen, Zhen Fan, and Tao Xiang We introduce a tree tensor network (TTN) impurity solver that enables highly efficient and accurate real-time simulations of quantum impurity models. By decomposing a noninteracting bath Hamiltonian into a Cayley tree, the method provides a tensor-network representation that naturally captures the m… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195144] Published Thu May 21, 2026

item.title: 联合学习谓词与动作实现零样本技能组合
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Benedict Quartey, Sebastian Castro, Eric Rosen, Wil Thomason, George Konidaris, Stefanie Tellex
模仿学习使机器人从专家示例学习复杂行为，但现有方法难以零样本泛化到已知技能的新组合。作者提出谓词动作技能PACTS，将技能建模为动作轨迹与谓词信念轨迹的联合生成过程，在单一模型内产生连贯的动作-结果推演。联合生成机制同时提升动作生成与谓词分类性能，并利用在线谓词预测作为符号接口，通过规划实现学习技能的零样本组合与执行监控。

item.title: 基于重要性平滑的深度状态空间模型高效学习
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 John-Joseph Brady, Nikolas Nusken, Yunpeng Li
该工作提出并行变分蒙特卡洛（PVMC）训练方法，弥合深度状态空间模型（DSSM）两大训练范式——变分自编码与序列蒙特卡洛（SMC）反向传播——之间的鸿沟。PVMC可稳健训练判别式与生成式DSSM任务，通过避免SMC前向传递的序列性瓶颈，在现代硬件上实现高效并行。基线实验表明，该方法达到或超越最优性能，且训练速度较最快竞争SMC方法提升10倍。该框架为大规模深度状态空间系统的统计建模提供了新的可扩展训练途径。
